finding_results.notes: Removed chapter/cycle meta language and replaced it with in-world memory anchors (the trust handoff and the Jamsil panic aftermath) so the burner reference stays diegetic.

Rebuilt the temptation/refusal beat with tactile and sensory detail (pulse contact, wet nylon, rain/oil/mint, prior physical habit) and made the refusal cost explicit in her body (pain, panic rebound, loss of sleep/heat).
Collapsed the multi-message stack into one dominant pressure event (Hyejin's urgent call about the locker) and closed on immediate concrete action.

Strengthened idiolect separation in the Mercury meeting: Ina's lines are shorter/concrete/high-contraction, Kwon keeps formal inevitability rhetoric, legal/comms interjections are terse tactical turns, and an interruption with an incomplete line now lands in-scene.

Differentiated secondary speaker idiolects in the 69-89 exchange: Song now speaks in bounded analytic ranges and model qualifiers; Bae uses blunt tactical, consequence-first language with colloquial pressure; Park uses legal narrowing language requiring scope and liability framing. Preserved scene outcome and leverage flow.
finding_results.summary: Core committee exchange now carries distinct, attributable voices under pressure instead of interchangeable procedural speech.

finding_results.notes: Inserted three distributed Ina-internal beats (somatic tension, tactical self-correction, and consequence memory) across the meeting exchange so the passage no longer runs as external-only briefing dialogue.
Replaced repeated mouth/jaw tightening reactions with varied embodied cues (hard breath/face drag and tool-case grip/latch click), leaving a single mouth-twist reaction in the dock beat.

pass_summaries.summary: All packet findings were addressed in-place: process leakage removed, intimate refusal de-sanitized with embodied consequence, endbeat refocused to one urgent hook with immediate action, and meeting dialogue differentiated by voice/register with interruption pressure.
Revised chapter 11 dialogue cadence/idiolect pass completed with the required committee voice-separation fix and no heading or continuity contract breaks introduced.

Applied targeted prose/copyedit revisions to chapter 11: restored close-third interior cadence in the meeting sequence and removed repeated facial-microreaction tics in the dock confrontation while preserving scene pressure, character voice, and unsanitized consequence detail.

summary: Merged three-pass revision report (FIXED=7, PARTIAL=0, UNRESOLVED=0).
